name: U-2
range:

max_stats:

description: The U-2 was a Soviet multipurpose biplane created in 1927 under the direction of N. N. Polikarpov.

name: I-16
range:
Takeoff run - 210 meters.
Rate of climb - 14.5 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 428 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 9100 meters.
Dive limit speed - 700 km/h.
description: The I-16, “sixteenth fighter” — colloquially “Ishak” or “Ishachok” — was a Soviet single-engine monoplane fighter of the 1930s, created by Nikolai Nikolayevich Polikarpov’s team at the OGPU design bureau. It was the world’s first mass-produced monoplane fighter with retractable landing gear. It was the most numerous fighter of the interwar period in the world: by the beginning of World War II, more than six thousand I-16 aircraft had been built.

name: Il-2
range:
Takeoff run - 450 meters.
Rate of climb - 6.2 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 406 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 9500 meters.
Dive limit speed - 620 km/h.
weapons:
VYa-23 cannon, 2 × 23 mm.
2 × 7.62 mm ShKAS machine guns.
6 hardpoints
description: The Il-2 was a Soviet ground-attack aircraft of World War II, created at OKB-240 under the direction of aircraft designer Sergey Ilyushin. It was the most mass-produced combat aircraft in aviation history, with more than 36 thousand units built.

name: MiG-17
range:
Takeoff run - 475 meters.
Rate of climb - 41.2 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1091 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 15500 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.1.

37 mm N-37D cannon.

description: The MiG-17 “Mikoyan-Gurevich” was a supersonic fighter produced in the Soviet Union from 1952 and used by air forces around the world. The MiG-17 was produced under license in China as the Shenyang J-5 and in Poland as the PZL-Mielec Lim-6. The MiG-17 is still used by the North Korean Air Force and took part in combat operations in the Middle East and Asia.

name: MiG-23
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 151.7 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2341 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.4.
description: The MiG-23, product “23-11”, NATO reporting name: Flogger, was a Soviet third-generation multirole fighter with variable-sweep wings, developed by OKB-155. It was produced in large series, entered service with fighter aviation and air defense aviation units, and was actively exported abroad.

name: MiG-27
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 137.8 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2183 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 15000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.8.
description: The MiG-27, product 32-25, NATO reporting name: Flogger-D, was a Soviet supersonic third-generation fighter-bomber with variable-sweep wings. It had a single-engine powerplant. It was intended for strikes against moving and stationary ground and air targets. It could carry tactical nuclear weapons.

name: Su-27
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 185.8 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2402 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.35.
description: The Su-27, factory code T-10S; NATO reporting name: Flanker-B, was a Soviet all-weather supersonic fourth-generation heavy fighter developed by the Sukhoi Design Bureau and intended to gain air superiority. The aircraft is also used for missile and bomb strikes against ground targets. It is capable of carrying tactical nuclear munitions.

name: Su-33
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 168.2 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2382 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.35.
description: The Su-33, factory code T-10K; formerly known as the Su-27K; NATO reporting name: Flanker-D, is a Soviet/Russian carrier-based fourth-generation fighter developed for the Russian Navy by the Sukhoi Design Bureau under the direction of Mikhail Simonov.

description: The Albatros D.III was a German biplane fighter used in World War I and one of the most successful fighters of the war.

range:

max_stats:
Maximum speed - 152 km/h.

description: The Albatros D.III was a German biplane fighter used in World War I and one of the most successful fighters of the war.

name: Bf 109 B-1
range:
Takeoff run - 210 meters.
Rate of climb - 10.2 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 465 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 9000 meters.
Dive limit speed - 790 km/h.
description: The Bf 109 B-1 was a single-engine piston low-wing fighter that served with the Luftwaffe and the air forces of various countries for almost 30 years. Depending on the modification, it was used as a day fighter, including specialized low-altitude versions and a family of high-altitude fighter variants, a day and night interceptor, a fighter-attack aircraft, a fighter-bomber, a fighter-reconnaissance aircraft, and an unarmed reconnaissance aircraft.

name: Fw 190 A-1
range:
Takeoff run - 450 meters.
Rate of climb - 14.1 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 652 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 13228 meters.
Dive limit speed - 912 km/h.
weapons:
2 × 20 mm MG FF/M cannons.
4 × 7.92 mm MG 17 machine guns.
description: The Fw 190 A-1 was a German single-seat, single-engine piston monoplane fighter that served with the Luftwaffe during World War II. The Fw 190 was successfully used in various roles, including as a high-altitude interceptor, especially the Fw 190D, escort fighter, attack aircraft, and night fighter, and earned a reputation as a true “workhorse” of the Luftwaffe.

name: F-86K
range:
Takeoff run - 1000 meters.
Rate of climb - 56 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1111 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 15500 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.25.
description: The F-86K was an American first-generation interceptor fighter developed by North American in 1953. The first YF-86K prototype took off on July 15, 1954. It was produced in the United States and Italy and was widely exported to NATO member states as an interceptor.

name: F-104G
range:
Takeoff run - 850 meters.
Rate of climb - 260 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2387 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.3.
description: The F-104G Starfighter was an American all-weather second-generation fighter-bomber developed by Lockheed Corporation. The G modification became the most numerous of all F-104 versions produced, and in West Germany the aircraft were first sold directly from the United States, after which production was established in Germany itself.

name: F-4F
range:
Takeoff run - 850 meters.
Rate of climb - 192 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2140 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.45.
description: The McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II was a two-seat jet fighter-bomber, later classified as a tactical fighter, of the third generation. In the early 1970s, the West German Air Force decided to purchase a batch of Phantoms from the United States to replace the rapidly aging F-104 Starfighter.

name: Su-22M4
range:
Takeoff run - 850 meters.
Rate of climb - 190 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2052 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 19500 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.15.
description: The Su-22M4 was an export variant of the Soviet Su-17M4 fighter-bomber, produced from 1983 to 1990. To strengthen its strike aviation capabilities, the German Democratic Republic received 48 Su-22M4 aircraft along with 10 Su-22UM3K trainers from 1984 to 1987, which were assigned to two squadrons: the 77th Fighter-Bomber Squadron (JBS77) and the 28th Naval Aviation Squadron (MFG-28).

name: MiG-29G
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 315 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2452 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.35.
description: The MiG-29G refers to MiG-29 aircraft, specifically the MiG-29 9-12A and MiG-29UB 9-51 of the German Air Force, modernized to NATO standards. On the MiG-29G and MiG-29GT aircraft, identification and navigation systems were modified to NATO standards, satellite navigation receivers were installed, and engine modifications were carried out.

name: Airco DH.4
range:
Takeoff run - 180 meters.
Rate of climb - 3.9 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 230 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 6700 meters.
Dive limit speed - 240 km/h.
weapons:
7.7 mm synchronized Vickers machine gun.

Up to 210 kg of bombs in bomb bays and on external mounts.
description: The Airco DH.4 was a British two-seat, single-engine biplane bomber widely produced in the United States under the name Liberty DH.4. It was notable for its high speed, range, and armament for its time. It was used as a day bomber and reconnaissance aircraft. It was the only American aircraft to take part in combat operations during World War I.

name: Boeing PW-9
range:
Takeoff run - 180 meters.
Rate of climb - 5.3 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 248 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 4270 meters.
Dive limit speed - 270 km/h.
weapons:
2 × 7.62 mm machine guns.
Capability to carry light bombs under the wings.
description: The Boeing PW-9 was one of the first American production fighters of the 1920s. It was developed based on the German Fokker D.VII and became a foundation for the development of U.S. fighter aviation during the interwar period. It was used mainly by army aviation and became an important step in Boeing’s rise as a leading U.S. aircraft manufacturer.

name: Boeing P-26 Peashooter
range:
Takeoff run - 170 meters.
Rate of climb - 12 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 377 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 8500 meters.
Dive limit speed - 508 km/h.
weapons:

description: The Boeing P-26 Peashooter was the first American-built fighter with an all-metal fuselage, as well as the first monoplane to enter service with the U.S. Air Force. It was designed and built by Boeing.

name: B-29 Superfortress
range:
Takeoff run - 1100 meters.
Rate of climb - 5.5 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 575 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 9700 meters.
Dive limit speed - 644 km/h.
weapons:
12 × 12.7 mm Browning M2 machine guns.
1 × 20 mm cannon.
Bomb load up to 9000 kg.
description: The B-29 Superfortress was one of the most advanced heavy bombers of World War II. It was equipped with pressurized compartments, an air-conditioning system, and remotely controlled gun turrets. It became famous as the aircraft that dropped the atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki.

name: F-104 Starfighter
range:
Takeoff run - 1180 meters.
Rate of climb - 235 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2149 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 18900 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.3.
weapons:
20 mm M61A1 cannon.
2 × AIM-9B Sidewinder air-to-air missiles.
description: The F-104 Starfighter was an American all-weather single-seat interceptor fighter. The F-104 was developed by Lockheed based on experience from the Korean War.

name: F-4 Phantom II
range:
Takeoff run - 850 meters.
Rate of climb - 172 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2288 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.45.
description: The F-4 Phantom II was a two-seat third-generation jet fighter-bomber and the first production version of the aircraft for the U.S. Air Force.

name: A-10 Thunderbolt II
range:
Takeoff run - 700 meters.
Rate of climb - 25.5 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 676 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 12497 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 0.8.
description: The A-10 Thunderbolt II is an American subsonic attack aircraft designed for close air support of troops on the battlefield. After the end of World War II, the focus of American military aircraft manufacturing shifted toward tactical aviation capable of delivering nuclear weapons.

name: F-117 Nighthawk
range:
Takeoff run - 1200 meters.
Rate of climb - 15.2 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1040 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 13700 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 0.92.
description: The F-117 Nighthawk was the world’s first production stealth aircraft. It was intended for covert penetration of air defense zones and the destruction of high-value targets. It was built according to low-observability principles using an angular shape and radar-absorbent coatings. It had no onboard radar and no weapons for air combat. It took part in conflicts in Panama, the Persian Gulf, Yugoslavia, and Iraq.

name: B-2 Spirit
range:
Takeoff run - 1400 meters.
Rate of climb - 9.1 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1010 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 15000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 0.95.
weapons: 2 internal bomb bays with a total payload of up to 18000 kg.
description: The B-2 Spirit is a strategic stealth bomber. It was designed using a “flying wing” configuration, which, together with radar-absorbent materials, makes it extremely difficult to detect by radar. It has the highest unit cost in aviation history, at 1 billion dollars. It is a primary carrier of nuclear and precision-guided conventional weapons in the U.S. Air Force.

name: Sopwith Camel
range:
Takeoff run - 300 meters.
Rate of climb - 5.5 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 199 km/h.
Dive limit speed - 240 km/h.
description: The Sopwith Camel was a British single-seat fighter of World War I, known for its excellent maneuverability among aircraft of that era. It was developed by the Sopwith Aviation Company.

name: Spitfire Mk.Ia

max_stats:

description: The Spitfire Mk.Ia was the first production version of the British single-engine fighter of World War II. It was developed in 1934–1936 by a team of designers at Supermarine Aviation Works led by Reginald Mitchell.

description: The Spitfire Mk.IIb was a modification of the British single-engine fighter of World War II, developed by a team of designers at Supermarine Aviation Works led by Reginald Mitchell in 1934–1936. It featured new armament and the new Merlin XII engine.

name: Hunter F.1
range:
Takeoff run - 820 meters.
Rate of climb - 55 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1108 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 14000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.6.
description: The Hawker Hunter F.1 modification entered service with the Royal Air Force in 1954 as an interceptor.

name: Hawker Siddeley Harrier
range:
Takeoff run - 550 meters.
Rate of climb - 87 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1102 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 12192 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.25.
description: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier was a British fighter-attack aircraft with vertical takeoff and landing capability. The Harrier was developed from the experimental Hawker Siddeley P.1127 Kestrel aircraft. The Harrier was the first attack aircraft with vertical/short takeoff and landing capability, VTOL/STOVL, and the only truly successful vertical takeoff and landing fighter.

name: Sea Harrier FRS.1
range:
Takeoff run - 550 meters.
Rate of climb - 87 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 1159 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 12192 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 1.25.
description: The Sea Harrier FRS.1 was an aircraft based on the Harrier GR.3 with several modifications: it received a new raised cockpit with a bubble canopy for improved visibility and an elongated nose section to house a radar.

name: Tornado F.3
range:
Takeoff run - 800 meters.
Rate of climb - 191 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2308 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 13000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.32.
description: The Tornado F.3 was a European interceptor fighter with variable-sweep wings, developed by the international consortium Panavia Aircraft GmbH. The aircraft’s first flight took place on November 20, 1985, and the fighters began entering service with Saudi Arabia, Italy, and the United Kingdom as early as 1986.

name: Typhoon FGR.4
range:
Takeoff run - 750 meters.
Rate of climb - 315 meters/sec.
max_stats:
Maximum speed - 2149 km/h.
Maximum altitude - 16000 meters.
Maximum Mach number - Mach 2.46.
description: The Eurofighter Typhoon FGR.4 is a European 4++ generation multirole fighter. Development of the aircraft began in 1983 under the Future European Fighter Aircraft, FEFA, program with the participation of the United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, Spain, and France. Production contracts were signed in September 1998.
